human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for financial-services servicing boundary discipline, source quality, complaint and billing-error handling, suspicious-activity escalation posture, commercialization notes, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to financial-services customer account servicing, complaint intake, payment or statement status handling, and escalation work tied to account, payment, transaction, complaint, and servicing records.

Authority sources cover CFPB Regulation E, Regulation X, and Regulation Z workflow rules; OCC customer-assistance guidance; FFIEC suspicious-activity guidance; FinCEN SAR-supporting-document constraints; Nacha ACH risk controls; and ISO 20022 artifact normalization.

Evaluation scenarios cover missing prerequisites, unsupported overrides, complaint repeat failures, suspicious-activity escalation, disclosure and reconciliation conflict, terminology fidelity, low-confidence escalation, public-source conflict, tenant adaptation, and adjacent-lane routing.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/financial-services/customer-account-servicing-specialist/ so customer account servicing stays separate from fraud, AML, underwriting, claims, legal, and servicing-operations decision authority.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around unsupported refunds, fee waivers, reinstatements, suspicious-activity communication, and legal or underwriting requests.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated customer account servicing specialist that supports evidence-backed service handling and policy-aware escalation.

Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and evaluation/functionality-map.json, and runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
